mechanism_narrative: High unemployment (UNRATE) signals economic weakness and reduced consumer spending, which historically leads to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate a prolonged downturn.
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation of unemployment with recessions
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are consistently positive in more than 60% of periods where UNRATE is above its 10-year moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate indicates monetary tightening, which increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:central bank policy impacts discount rates
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS has risen by more than 0.1pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth (RSAFS) decelerates significantly while unemployment (UNRATE) is high, it suggests severe consumer distress and a deep economic contraction, negatively impacting corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major GDP component
failure_condition: Periods of decelerating RSAFS and high UNRATE do not show a statistically significant negative impact on SPX 20d returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A strong recovery in housing starts (HOUST) after a downturn indicates renewed consumer and business confidence, stimulating broader economic activity and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:housing market often leads economic cycles
failure_condition: During identified post-recession recovery periods, SPX 60d returns are not significantly positive when HOUST is above 500k units (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing and economic activity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial output correlates with corporate earnings
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO month-over-month growth over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds a 3% annual rate, it signals an overheating economy that often leads to tighter monetary policy and reduced corporate profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:high inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate margins
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Robust employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with solid GDP expansion (GDPC1) indicates a healthy and expanding economy, providing a strong fundamental backdrop for corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:employment and GDP are key economic health indicators
failure_condition: Periods of simultaneous strong PAYEMS and GDPC1 growth do not exhibit significantly positive SPX 20d returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP (GDPC1) signals a recession, leading to widespread declines in corporate profits and investor confidence, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP contraction is a hallmark of recession
failure_condition: During officially declared recessionary periods, SPX 5d returns are not predominantly negative when GDPC1 shows contraction (positive returns in > 40% of triggered instances).
